scene_title: London. Before a tavern in Eastcheap

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: Prithee, honey-sweet husband, let me bring thee to Staines.

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: No; for my manly heart doth yearn.

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: Bardolph, be blithe: Nym, rouse thy vaunting veins:

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: Boy, bristle thy courage up; for Falstaff he is dead,

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: And we must yearn therefore.

lines.speaker: BARDOLPH
lines.text: Would I were with him, wheresome’er he is, either in heaven or in hell!

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: Nay, sure, he’s not in hell: he’s in Arthur’s bosom, if ever

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: man went to Arthur’s bosom. A’ made a finer end and went

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: away an it had been any christom child; a’ parted even just

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: between twelve and one, even at the turning o’ the tide: for

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: after I saw him fumble with the sheets and play with flowers

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: and smile upon his fingers’ ends, I knew there was but one

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: way; for his nose was as sharp as a pen, and a’

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: babbled of green fields. “How now, Sir John!” quoth I: “what, man!

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: be o’ good cheer.” So a’ cried out, “God, God, God!” three

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: or four times. Now I, to comfort him, bid him a’ should

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: not think of God; I hoped there was no need to trouble

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: himself with any such thoughts yet. So a’ bade me lay more

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: clothes on his feet: I put my hand into the bed and

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: felt them, and they were as cold as any stone; then I

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: felt to his knees, and they were as cold as any stone,

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: and so upward and upward, and all was as cold as any

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: stone.

lines.speaker: NYM
lines.text: They say he cried out of sack.

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: Ay, that a’ did.

lines.speaker: BARDOLPH
lines.text: And of women.

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: Nay, that a’ did not.

lines.speaker: BOY
lines.text: Yes, that a’ did; and said they were devils incarnate.

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: A’ could never abide carnation; ’twas a colour he never liked.

lines.speaker: BOY
lines.text: A’ said once, the devil would have him about women.

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: A’ did in some sort, indeed, handle women; but then he was

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: rheumatic, and talk’d of the whore of Babylon.

lines.speaker: BOY
lines.text: Do you not remember, a’ saw a flea stick upon Bardolph’s nose,

lines.speaker: BOY
lines.text: and a’ said it was a black soul burning in hellfire?

lines.speaker: BARDOLPH
lines.text: Well, the fuel is gone that maintained that fire: that’s all the

lines.speaker: BARDOLPH
lines.text: riches I got in his service.

lines.speaker: NYM
lines.text: Shall we shog? the king will be gone from Southampton.

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: Come, let’s away. My love, give me thy lips.

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: Look to my chattels and my movables:

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: Let senses rule; the word is “Pitch and Pay:”

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: Trust none;

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: For oaths are straws, men’s faiths are wafer-cakes,

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: And holdfast is the only dog, my duck:

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: Therefore, Caveto be thy counsellor.

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: Go, clear thy crystals. Yoke-fellows in arms,

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: Let us to France; like horse-leeches, my boys,

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: To suck, to suck, the very blood to suck!

lines.speaker: BOY
lines.text: And that’s but unwholesome food, they say.

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: Touch her soft mouth, and march.

lines.speaker: BARDOLPH
lines.text: Farewell, hostess. Kissing her.

lines.speaker: NYM
lines.text: I cannot kiss, that is the humour of it; but, adieu.

lines.speaker: PISTOL
lines.text: Let housewifery appear: keep close, I thee command.

lines.speaker: HOSTESS
lines.text: Farewell; adieu.

stage_directions.text: Enter Pistol, Hostess, Nym, Bardolph and Boy.

glosses.definition: a town on the Thames west of London — stop on the road to Southampton
Pistol's grief — 'yearn' here = ache with sorrow
boastful blood-vessels — Pistol's bombastic diction
Sir John Falstaff's death — imported from the end of Henry IV Part 2; reported, not staged
the Hostess's malapropism for 'Abraham's bosom' (Luke 16:22) — the resting-place of the righteous after death
a better death
the Hostess's malapropism for 'chrisom child' — a baby in its christening-cloth, proverbial for innocence
the moment of tidal change — old folk-belief held that dying men passed with the tide
pluck absently at the bed-linen — a classical sign of the dying

glosses.phrase: nose was as sharp as a pen
glosses.definition: his nose had grown pointed and thin — a traditional symptom of approaching death
famous textual crux — Theobald's emendation of the Folio's nonsense; conveys Falstaff in delirium remembering pastoral scenes
Falstaff's deathbed cry — the Hostess misunderstands, thinking he needs comforting away from religion
spoke with regret of sack — the sweet sherry Falstaff was famous for loving
the color carnation/flesh-pink — the Hostess puns on 'carnal'; she insists Falstaff never cared for that color
the Hostess's malapropism — she means 'lunatic' or 'choleric'; 'rheumatic' = catarrhal, not relevant
apocalyptic image from Revelation 17 — Protestant rhetoric used it for the Catholic Church
Falstaff's joke about Bardolph's notoriously red nose — a flea on it burns like a damned soul
get going; move off (dialect)
pay cash; literally 'throw down the money' — Pistol's motto for tavern credit
promises are worthless as straws in the wind
flimsy as communion wafers — faith is fragile
gripping tightly — 'the only dog' proverbially means the one virtue to keep
Latin: 'beware' — Pistol's Latinate motto for caution
wipe your eyes — 'crystals' = crystalline tears/eyes
yoked together in military service
large bloodsucking worms — Pistol's image of army camp-followers as parasites
French: goodbye — a mildly affected farewell
proper wifely management — Pistol orders the Hostess to keep house while he's at war

annotations.title: Falstaff's death — narrative necessity
annotations.body: The death of Falstaff, reported with unguarded tenderness by the Hostess, is one of the most-loved passages in Shakespeare and one of the most necessary. Sir John Falstaff had been the comic center of Henry IV Parts 1 and 2 — the fat knight whose drunken wit made Prince Hal's youth recognizably human. At the end of Part 2, Henry (now crowned) publicly rejected him. Part of that play's bargain with the audience was the Epilogue's promise that Falstaff would return in Henry V. Shakespeare went back on that promise: Falstaff does not appear. The reasons are speculative — possibly a dispute with the actor Will Kemp, possibly a recognition that Falstaff's comic register is incompatible with the martial tone of Henry V. Whatever the cause, Shakespeare had to remove the character, and he does it with exquisite misdirection: the Hostess's mangled syntax, the famous 'babbled of green fields,' the detail of Falstaff fumbling with the sheets at the turning of the tide. Falstaff dies in the play's wings; the audience inherits the grief without ever seeing the body.
sources: J. Dover Wilson, The Fortunes of Falstaff (1943)
Harold Bloom, Shakespeare: The Invention of the Human (1998)

annotations.title: 'A' babbled of green fields' — Theobald's textual miracle
annotations.body: The phrase 'a' babbled of green fields,' one of the most resonant lines in Shakespeare, does not appear in any original text. The First Folio (1623) reads 'a Table of greene fields,' which is nonsense. The eighteenth-century editor Lewis Theobald conjectured in 1733 that a compositor had misread Shakespeare's 'a bablde' (abbreviated for 'a babbled') as 'a Table.' Theobald's emendation is so accepted that modern editions simply print it; few lines of Shakespeare are so universally cited and so purely editorial. The reading gives us a dying Falstaff in delirious peace, remembering pastoral — an image that retroactively humanizes the whole Eastcheap world. It is worth noting that this is what textual editing, at its best, can do: it can recover a line that makes a whole scene sing.
sources: Stanley Wells & Gary Taylor, William Shakespeare: A Textual Companion (1987)

trials.prompt: Why does Falstaff not appear in Henry V, given he was the star of Henry IV Parts 1 and 2?
options: Shakespeare wrote him into the play but censors cut it

Because the martial register of Henry V has no place for his comic-unruly world — and his rejection at the end of Henry IV Part 2 had already severed him from the now-king. His death is reported, not staged
Because the actor who played Falstaff had died
Because the Queen disliked the character

trials.prompt: What is the textual status of 'a' babbled of green fields' (line 14)?
options: It appears in all original editions
The Folio (1623) has nonsense ('a Table of greene fields'); the famous line is an 18th-century editorial conjecture by Lewis Theobald that has become universally accepted
It is only in the First Quarto and cut from the Folio
It was added by Samuel Johnson in 1765

trials.prompt: The Hostess's speech about Falstaff's death is full of malapropisms. 'Arthur's bosom' is her version of what?
options: 'Abraham's bosom' — the biblical resting-place of the righteous after death (Luke 16:22)
